etymology.description: 言(言) (yán, "speech; words") + 己(己) (jǐ, "self") = speech related to oneself -> final form 記
etymology.interpretation: The character 記 (jì, "record") combines speech 言(言), indicating communication or words, with the phonetic 己(己), referring to oneself. This combination suggests the idea of recording or remembering words related to oneself or one's experiences.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 記 was simplified to 记 by replacing 言(言, "speech") with the simpler 讠, reducing stroke count while maintaining the semantic component.

examples.chinese: 我每天写日记(我每天寫日記)。
examples.english: I write a diary every day.

examples.chinese: 请记住这条重要的规则(請記住這條重要的規則)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng jì zhù zhè tiáo zhòngyào de guīzé.

examples.chinese: 他把电话号码记在本子上(他把電話號碼記在本子上)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ diànhuà hàomǎ jì zài běnzi shàng.
examples.english: He recorded the phone number in the notebook.
